spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (navigator., document.querySelector, PaymentRequest) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (2 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (confirmation, fits, flow, integration, merchant, payment, secure, webauthn).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 14 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "Adds a new static method to the Payment Request that allows web developers to get the capabilities of the browser's implementation of Secure Payment Confirmation.  This helps web developers to easily ". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Adds a new static method to the Payment Request that allows web developers to get the capabilities of the browser's implementation of Secure Payment Confirmation.  This helps web developers to easily know what capabilities are available for") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://w3c.github.io/secure-payment-confirmation/#sctn-secure-payment-confirmation-capabilities to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~1424 chars of script across 2 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
